name: Jay Inslee
subject: Trumpcare is back!
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington
body:
President Trump might not care about your health, but we can show Republicans we won't give up.

| [](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=3&ms=MTA5NTQ1MTES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE2MDM4MDkxOAS2&mt=1&rt=0) | **BREAKING NEWS:
House Republicans just passed Trump's newest health care bill** | [TAKE ACTION »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTA5NTQ1MTES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE2MDM4MDkxOAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
---
Friend,
Trumpcare is back -- with a vengeance. And it will hurt millions of working people across Washington.
President Trump and Paul Ryan rushed their dangerous health care bill through the House before we could even learn of the real impacts it will have on people across Washington.
And they did that for one reason: _They know this bill will harm millions of people, but they desperately needed their failing President to have a win._
Their new policies make it more difficult and expensive to get health care if you are a woman, eliminates protections for pre-existing health conditions, and does away with important Medicare expansions. Let me break that down for you: If you are a woman, if you are already sick, if you are older, if you live in rural parts of our country, then Republicans in the House don't care about your health.
**Luckily, there 's still something we can do, if we act fast. The bill will now go to the Senate, and we have a chance to make sure it stops there -- but we have to put the pressure on NOW.**
[**Join me in telling Republicans in the Senate: Don 't pass this bill and endanger the lives of hundreds of thousands of Washingtonians!**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTA5NTQ1MTES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE2MDM4MDkxOAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
President Trump is showing again and again that he will put his billionaire friends before working Americans.
You and I know that there is no basis other than discrimination to make health care more expensive for women. We know that older citizens need easier access to less expensive health care in order to stay as healthy as possible. And we know it is simply cruel-hearted to turn away people who need health care the most.
President Trump knows this too. The difference is he doesn't care. **And that is why we have to show Republicans in the Senate that we care -- and we demand that the stop this dangerous bill.**
[**Sign on today and tell the Senate to stop Trumpcare in its tracks!**](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=2&ms=MTA5NTQ1MTES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE2MDM4MDkxOAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
Thank you.
Very truly yours,
Jay Inslee
[TAKE ACTION »](http://links.e.jayinslee.com/ctt?kn=4&ms=MTA5NTQ1MTES1&r=MTM2NjM5NDY2ODI1S0&b=0&j=MTE2MDM4MDkxOAS2&mt=1&rt=0)
---

Contributions or gifts to Jay Inslee for Washington are not tax deductible. | Paid for by Jay Inslee for Washington

name: Roy Cooper
subject: We have to fight back
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Cooper for North Carolina
body:
At least 24 million, friends -- that's how many people would lose their health coverage under the House Republican health care bill.
|
[](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202f46/2fde7c66/43f87fa9/15690060/1305999656/VEsH/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-with-roy-and-fight-for-healthcare?utm_campaign=em17_170505_health_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em17_170505_health_adv_gq_ful__rc&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")
---
At least 24 million, friends, That's how many people would lose their health coverage under the House Republican health care bill. Costs for seniors would go up. People with pre-existing conditions would no longer be protected. This is wrong and it will hurt North Carolinians. **[I'm going to continue to fight for all North Carolinians, and I'm urging our two U.S. senators to stand up and vote "no" on this disastrous bill. If you're with me, add your name here.](http://act.roycooper.com/page/m/1202f46/2fde7c66/43f87fa9/15690060/1305999656/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZHU1ZKVFZFNUJUVVVsSlE9PSI6IiIsIkpTVk1RVk5VVGtGTlJTVWwiOiIiLCJKU1ZhU1ZBbEpRPT0iOiIyODQ1NyIsIkpTVkZUVUZKVENVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIn0=/ "http://act.roycooper.com/page/s/stand-with-roy-and-fight-for-healthcare?utm_campaign=em17_170505_health_adv_gq_ful__rc&utm_medium=email&utm_source=cooper&source=em17_170505_health_adv_gq_ful__rc&firstname=&lastname=&zip=28457&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com")** This is simple -- North Carolinians deserve better than what we're getting from Washington. The folks across this state who just want to keep their health insurance need someone who will stand up for them. And if the bill passed by House Republicans yesterday becomes law, governors will be the last line of defense for working and middle-class families. As governor, I will continue to fight every single day to protect their health care. name: Patrick Murphy
subject: Yep, they did it
body:
| |
---
| | [](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2ede/738133e4/3325a237/7f85a74f/3037952348/VEsH/ "https://www.murphyforflorida.com")
---
| Dear friends, After twisting enough arms and making some truly life-threatening concessions, the House passed its version of an Obamacare repeal yesterday. They named it the "American Health Care Act," but I bet you'd be hard-pressed to find anyone in America whose coverage it actually improves. Instead, the bill makes things worse. Much worse. If it passes the Senate and is signed into law by President Trump, the AHCA would:
* Take away healthcare coverage for 24 million Americans
* Eliminate protections for those with pre-existing conditions
* Remove billions in Medicaid coverage
* Give even more tax breaks to those making over $250,000 a year

And who knows what else it could do? After complaining for seven years that Obamacare was "rammed through" and "forced" onto Americans, House Republicans voted on _their_ legislation without a single hearing or even a new Congressional Budget Office score. There is simply no way to know exactly how much their plan would cost and who it would affect. Hypocrisy is all too frequent in politics these days, but this is _off the_ _charts_. I happened to be in Washington yesterday when the GOP decided to put the health care of millions of Americans in danger. Despite loud opposition to the AHCA, a historically unpopular president, and little in the way of public input, partisan politics won out _yet again_ over the well-being of the middle class. It's all the more reason why we need a sharp change of direction. Every Republican who voted for this disastrous legislation has a lot to answer for. I hope you'll join me in holding them accountable in 2018. \-- Patrick _**[P.S. We've got a real shot at taking back the House next year -- will you make a grassroots donation to help us flip key seats?](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2ede/738133e4/3325a237/7f85a748/3037952348/VEsE/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/170505pem?refcode=link")**_ [CONTRIBUTE](https://secure.murphyforflorida.com/page/m/10ae2ede/738133e4/3325a237/7f85a749/3037952348/VEsF/ "https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/170505pem?refcode=button") |

Since Tom emailed after yesterday's vote, thousands of Democrats have stepped up to help us beat the Republicans who voted to take away our health care.

Lindsey

subject: The Party of Purpose Delivers Again
body:
| | |
---
| |
---
| |
---
|
---
**_The Party of Purpose Delivers Again_**
The Party of Purpose delivered again this week - both in Indiana and in Washington, D.C. Governor Eric Holcomb announced 2,000 new jobs would be coming to the Hoosier State thanks to a new technology firm, Infosys, choosing Indiana for the first round of it's U.S. expansion. Two days later in our nation's capital, Hoosiers were at the forefront of making good on our promise to repeal and replace Obamacare. Vice President Mike Pence and CMS Administrator Seema Verma were integral to the discussions and every Republican member of our U.S. House delegation voted yes. This is a great step forward.

And we're going to celebrate these successes and the many successes yet to come Monday night at our 2017 Spring Dinner with Donald Trump Jr. The event is now SOLD OUT. A huge achievement in a non-election year, but a clear indication that Indiana's Party of Purpose is getting stronger and stronger by the day.

For those of you attending the event, please see the final list of times below. See you there!

Meet & Greet with Donald Trump Jr. - 4:45pm
VIP Reception with Donald Trump Jr. - 5:30pm
General Reception - 5:30pm
Dinner - 6:45pm
_Please note the Meet & Greet and VIP Reception are open only to guests ticketed for those events._

\- Kyle

(Photo: Chairman Hupfer speaking to a packed house at the monthly Downtown GOP Club meeting in Indianapolis on Wednesday night.)
|
---
| | _**Governor Holcomb's Week That Was**_
---
|
---
| Governor Eric Holcomb made national - and international(!) - news Tuesday morning when he announced global technology firm Infosys will create 2,000 new jobs in central Indiana. The [governor appeared with Infosys CEO](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=cffc92d1a6&e=1acd9bb3d6) Dr. Vishal Sikka on CNBC to discuss this huge news.

The governor also received a huge seal of approval from the Northwest Indiana Times for his commitment to the Region shown in the latest legislative session and his numerous trips to the area. [Read about it here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=4f2949393c&e=1acd9bb3d6).
---
|
---
| [ ](http://indgop.us11.list-manage.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=9b44e60758&e=1acd9bb3d6)
---
| _**Secretary of State Connie Lawson Announces Re-election Campaign**_
Secretary of State Connie Lawson this week announced her decision to seek re-election next year. This is GREAT news for Hoosiers.

Secretary Lawson is a remarkable public servant and the Indiana Republican Party fully supports her and will be fully invested in her re-election efforts. Over the years, Secretary Lawson and her team have worked hard to protect Hoosiers from unscrupulous scam artists, to modernize the Secretary of State’s office and to ensure the integrity of our electoral process by making voting safer and easier. It’s a record of proven leadership delivering proven results. She is an outstanding member of our statewide team and one of our top goals in 2018 will be to ensure that continues to be the case for years to come.

You can watch Secretary Lawson's [video announcement here](http://indgop.us11.list-manage1.com/track/click?u=89fb3de929a99eecb88e6fb27&id=994fbb9dc0&e=1acd9bb3d6) or by clicking the image above.
---
|
---
|
---
| _**Hoosiers Lead the Way on Repealing & Replacing Obamacare**_
President Donald Trump and Congressional Republicans made a promise to repeal the government-controlled Affordable Care Act and replace it with patient-centered reforms. Yesterday’s vote was the first step in making good on that promise. It's important to remember that we wouldn't even be in the position of needing to repeal and replace Obamacare had it not been for Joe Donnelly voting for the job-killing, premium-hiking, chaos-creating bill in the first place.

You can read statements from each member of the Indiana Republican Congressional delegation who supported the American Health Care Act below.

**NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER** FRIDAY, May 5th |
---|---|---
---
---
---
| | _**A Note From Jeanie**_ | |
---
| Dear Derek,

It has been an eventful week here in New Hampshire and in Washington.
President Trump passed his 100 Day mark as President, the House Republicans repealed the disastrous Obamacare and voted for a responsible replacement, and the administration helped focus on America's small business during National Small Business week.
Here in New Hampshire, the State House and Senate have been working on a number of important issues including returning taxpayer dollars to NH communities, the passage of a cross border drug interdiction program, and a stronger rape shield law.
I appreciate how Republicans, from local activists to our highest elected official, Governor Sununu, are stepping up to hold and grow our Republican majorities. Even in an off-cycle year, the enthusiasm and commitment is exciting.

Thanks to the NH House PAC, significant financial support has been committed to Mark McLean (Hillsborough 44) and Matthew Plache (Carroll 6). Local activists Joan Fulton, Chris Ager, and Laura Gandia worked together to host a "packed-to-the rafters" house party for Mark McLean. Governor Sununu stopped in to support Mark’s efforts. Next week, another house party is planned for Matthew Plache in Wolfeboro. (see events section)
_**[Our fundraiser on May18th with special guest Kellyanne Conway](http://email.robly.com/wf/click?upn=gT2AZ1ZnBy-2FrecDREa9-2BbVCInQgtZVu409EhFI-2BClEJcu2CUmW89Ku819D2SduyT_enNSH-2FxHAAqXgxiHRFihpX91d75ERAkpfg4XQfmYhCFb47pW8iDRAYLsM9sf9wIa92fG-2FsxU68zXzzMA2nO2VCunl2bm-2FhLXsWS8P3XITs28GUU7Mnlu37POPaWv9myv4YWBbFU9plZi0-2B42tZfBaUujH4mxA3e6jOiyDa5q6HKz45-2B17g6DUNL-2BnIMAR30M-2F0LKVvSVj5-2BHBuZV59sQQ1J1GrC4RhwHVxXtjHxZKSPjdi3prUmf0QErCxLugAaYlS9DB1pXNIV7BteHY2K588sVbP8YCuM6y1JJL6WT-2BpGtbS4fyp6PiTxALCUGaFAlKWNYzDV6hoxtir1rSnB7o9-2BRPsTbZl5kJeuSCXnPdSwD2xk3D-2BKoOlNxith3lCQAj2HQ6cYpzPfFHEu-2BXBlewMOHKRV6UVPbXLlrxHqX2-2FYz2f2s6nYDkftXouo-2BLzkxVGtbBDjJUOqxAqXADk6iGw-3D-3D)**_ is helping the Party build the operation we need to make sure we grow our majorities in Concord so that we can continue to see this positive change that helps our state flourish. It will also help us jumpstart our plan to win back both Congressional districts so that New Hampshire has a voice in Washington again.
Thank you for your continued support and positive feedback!
Sincerely, _Jeanie_

| _**House Republicans**_
**House Majority Pleased With House Approval For Infrastructure Aid Package**
SB 38, making an appropriation for local highway aid and municipal bridge aid, passed the House today on a voice vote. House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack) released the following statement in response to the passage of SB38.
House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued the following statement:
“This bill will send $30 million back to municipalities for the purpose of constructing, reconstructing, and maintaining roads and $6.8 million in bridge aid. This aid package comes in addition to the dollars that flow back to communities through existing formulas. This session we’ve made it a priority to send money back to cities and towns. We are proud we can make local needs a priority. The passage of this bill further strengthens our commitment to improving our state’s infrastructure. The health of our roads and bridges are important investment for our communities and local economies.”
* * *
**House Majority Leader Praises Strengthening NH’s Rape Shield Law**
House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack) offered the following statement in response to the passage of SB9, relative to the admissibility of proffered evidence in sexual assault cases. The bill passed the House by a 328 - 30 vote.
House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack):
“This is an important piece of legislation that protects the privacy of sexual assault victims, their families, and strengthens New Hampshire’s rape shield law. This will ensure that a victim’s sexual history will remain sealed from the jury, public, and press while not infringing upon a defendant's rights or jeopardizing due process.”
“Although in line with practices in New Hampshire, SB9 is a critical clarification in the law that will not leave victims of sexual assault forced to choose between their right to privacy and their right for justice. I commend the House for passing SB9, and I look forward to it being signed into law.”
|

---
---
| _**Senate Republicans**_
**Morse applauds House passage of bill returning taxpayer dollars to NH communities**
Concord, NH - Today, the House passed SB 38, which would send nearly $37 million in surplus Fiscal Year ’17 dollars back to taxpayers for community infrastructure, road and bridge projects.
Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem), the bill’s prime sponsor, issued the following statement after the vote:
“Improving New Hampshire’s aging infrastructure and numerous red list bridges is a priority for the State of New Hampshire. I applaud the House for voting to send $36.8 million in surplus FY ’17 funds back to our communities to make these necessary road and bridge repairs for the benefit of our citizens and small businesses throughout the state.”
“We have a responsibility to spend taxpayer dollars wisely and I believe sending back surplus taxpayer funds to our communities for the purpose of making critical community improvements is the right thing to do.” |
---
---
| **Daniels assures public hearing testimony to be considered in budget process**
Concord, NH - Today, the Senate Finance Committee held the public hearing on HB 144 and HB 517, bills which provide the foundation for the state budget for Fiscal Year 18-19.
Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford) issued the following statement: “I am pleased to see so many Granite Staters come out today to present their ideas, questions or concerns during the public hearing with the Senate Finance Committee as part of the regular budget process.”
“Our goals continue to include meeting the needs of New Hampshire’s most vulnerable citizens while spending only what we can afford. We will work hard to thoughtfully consider the testimony presented today as we continue to work through balancing the complex needs in our state.” |

clean_body: | | | 8 NEW HAMPSHIRE REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, May 5th | ---|---|--- | | A Note From Jeanie | | | Dear Derek, It has been an eventful week here in New Hampshire and in Washington. President Trump passed his 100 Day mark as President, the House Republicans repealed the disastrous Obamacare and voted for a responsible replacement, and the administration helped focus on America's small business during National Small Business week. Here in New Hampshire, the State House and Senate have been working on a number of important issues including returning taxpayer dollars to NH communities, the passage of a cross border drug interdiction program, and a stronger rape shield law. I appreciate how Republicans, from local activists to our highest elected official, Governor Sununu, are stepping up to hold and grow our Republican majorities. Even in an off-cycle year, the enthusiasm and commitment is exciting. Thanks to the NH House PAC, significant financial support has been committed to Mark McLean (Hillsborough 44) and Matthew Plache (Carroll 6). Local activists Joan Fulton, Chris Ager, and Laura Gandia worked together to host a "packed-to-the rafters" house party for Mark McLean. Governor Sununu stopped in to support Mark’s efforts. Next week, another house party is planned for Matthew Plache in Wolfeboro. (see events section) is helping the Party build the operation we need to make sure we grow our majorities in Concord so that we can continue to see this positive change that helps our state flourish. It will also help us jumpstart our plan to win back both Congressional districts so that New Hampshire has a voice in Washington again. Thank you for your continued support and positive feedback! Sincerely, Jeanie | | | Join the Grassroots Circle! | | The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. May 18 - 6:00 PM Radisson Hotel, 11 Tara Blvd, Nashua, NH To RSVP & for more information Sullivan County GOP Gala May 19 - 6:00 PM Mount Sunapee Resort, 1398 Route 103, Newbury, NH For More Information, | | | | | | Event Highlight | ---|---|--- | "Spring to Victory" Dinner with Special Guest Kellyanne Conway! | | We are hosting our first fundraising dinner of the year and we are so excited to have Kellyanne Conway as our special guest. Your participation in this event will be helping the Party continue to grow our campaign operation as we head into 2018. This dinner will be sold-out soon, so don't wait to purchase until the last minute! | ---|---|--- | | Photo of the Week | ---|---|--- Governor Chris Sununu speaking to attendees of the NHGOP Grassroots Communications Summit on Saturday | Republican Spotlight! | ---|---|--- | Thank you for all of your submissions! This week's spotlight shines on Laura Gandia of Litchfield! Laura is a leader in her community. She opened up her home and hosted an event in support of Mark McLean who us running for State Representative in the Special Election for Hillsborough County district 44. We thank Laura for her dedication to her Party and for all of her hard work in helping get Mark Elected. Who do you think should be in the spotlight in next week's newsletter? | | | Legislative Highlights | ---|---|--- | House Republicans House Majority Pleased With House Approval For Infrastructure Aid Package SB 38, making an appropriation for local highway aid and municipal bridge aid, passed the House today on a voice vote. House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack) released the following statement in response to the passage of SB38. House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack) issued the following statement: “This bill will send $30 million back to municipalities for the purpose of constructing, reconstructing, and maintaining roads and $6.8 million in bridge aid. This aid package comes in addition to the dollars that flow back to communities through existing formulas. This session we’ve made it a priority to send money back to cities and towns. We are proud we can make local needs a priority. The passage of this bill further strengthens our commitment to improving our state’s infrastructure. The health of our roads and bridges are important investment for our communities and local economies.” House Majority Leader Praises Strengthening NH’s Rape Shield Law House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack) offered the following statement in response to the passage of SB9, relative to the admissibility of proffered evidence in sexual assault cases. The bill passed the House by a 328 - 30 vote. House Majority Leader Richard Hinch (R-Merrimack): “This is an important piece of legislation that protects the privacy of sexual assault victims, their families, and strengthens New Hampshire’s rape shield law. This will ensure that a victim’s sexual history will remain sealed from the jury, public, and press while not infringing upon a defendant's rights or jeopardizing due process.” “Although in line with practices in New Hampshire, SB9 is a critical clarification in the law that will not leave victims of sexual assault forced to choose between their right to privacy and their right for justice. I commend the House for passing SB9, and I look forward to it being signed into law.” | | Senate Republicans Morse applauds House passage of bill returning taxpayer dollars to NH communities Concord, NH - Today, the House passed SB 38, which would send nearly $37 million in surplus Fiscal Year ’17 dollars back to taxpayers for community infrastructure, road and bridge projects. Senate President Chuck Morse (R-Salem), the bill’s prime sponsor, issued the following statement after the vote: “Improving New Hampshire’s aging infrastructure and numerous red list bridges is a priority for the State of New Hampshire. I applaud the House for voting to send $36.8 million in surplus FY ’17 funds back to our communities to make these necessary road and bridge repairs for the benefit of our citizens and small businesses throughout the state.” “We have a responsibility to spend taxpayer dollars wisely and I believe sending back surplus taxpayer funds to our communities for the purpose of making critical community improvements is the right thing to do.” | | Daniels assures public hearing testimony to be considered in budget process Concord, NH - Today, the Senate Finance Committee held the public hearing on HB 144 and HB 517, bills which provide the foundation for the state budget for Fiscal Year 18-19. Senate Finance Chair Gary Daniels (R-Milford) issued the following statement: “I am pleased to see so many Granite Staters come out today to present their ideas, questions or concerns during the public hearing with the Senate Finance Committee as part of the regular budget process.” “Our goals continue to include meeting the needs of New Hampshire’s most vulnerable citizens while spending only what we can afford. name: Elizabeth Warren
subject: I will fight my heart out

body:
The ACA repeal bill will now head to the Senate - and it's time to fight like your life is on the line.
---
[](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e74a/2b07114c/5268d29e/736e97cd/241848425/VEsH/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/warren-stop-repeal?source=PT-20170505-Warren-ProsHV")
Team,

The Republican Trumpcare bill isn't a health care bill. A bill that strips health care away from millions of people in order to fund an enormous tax break for the rich is not a health care bill.

Make no mistake: The bill that the House Republicans passed yesterday is the same cruel bill they tried to pass weeks ago. The only difference is that the new plan is even more brutal -- opening the door to discriminating against people with pre-existing conditions.

The American people -- Republicans and Democrats, independents and Trump voters -- despise this bill. They rejected it before. We'll reject it again.

**The ACA repeal bill will now head to the Senate -- and I promise you: I will fight my heart out to stop this monstrosity.**

But we need you -- yes, you team -- to speak out and fight. Call, write, email, post, tweet, and share your story. Talk about what's at stake for the people you love if they lose their health care.

[**Start by adding your name right now: tell Republicans not to repeal the ACA.**](https://secure.dscc.org/page/m/3954e74a/2b07114c/5268d29e/736e97cd/241848425/VEsE/ "https://secure.dscc.org/page/s/warren-stop-repeal?source=PT-20170505-Warren-ProsHV")

It's time to fight like your life is on the line -- because for millions of people, it is.

Thanks for being a part of this,

Elizabeth
